name: Bishr ibn Amr
title: Devoted Father
shortDescription: A loyal companion of the Imam who, despite hearing the news of his son's captivity in Rey, refused to leave the Imam and was martyred.
fullStory:
Bishr ibn Amr al-Hadhrami was one of the brave and sincere Shias of Kufa who reached Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan in Karbala before the battle began.

In the days leading up to Ashura, while he was in the Imam's camp, terrible news reached him: his son (Amr ibn Bishr) who was at the borders of Rey had been taken captive. Imam Hussain (AS), who always cared for his companions, told Bishr: 'You are free to go and strive for your son's release.'

But Bishr ibn Amr's response was recorded in history as one of the most beautiful manifestations of loyalty. He replied to the Imam: 'May the wild beasts of the desert eat me alive if I leave you alone among these enemies and go after my own affairs!'

The Imam, seeing his loyalty, gave him valuable garments to send for his son's release, but Bishr stayed in Karbala. On the day of Ashura, he bravely went to the battlefield and, after a manifest battle, joined the caravan of Karbala's martyrs.
quoteArabic: أَكَلَتْنِي إِذَنْ السِّباعُ حَيّاً إِنْ أَنَا فارَقْتُكَ يا أَباعَبْدِاللَّهِ...
quote: (His reply to the Imam:) May the wild beasts of the desert eat me alive if I leave you alone among these enemies...
